evidence: Predicate hierarchy (LemmaPredicate, TagPredicate, PosGroupPredicate, WordPredicate, AndPredicate, OrPredicate, NotPredicate) uses a perfectly uniform naming convention

HybridConllUProcessor and SinglePassConlluProcessor live in the same package (indexer.hybrid), both delegate to HybridIndexer, but are named along different axes: 'Hybrid' describes the output type while 'SinglePass' describes the algorithm
CoNLL-U is spelled three different ways across class names: ConllU (HybridConllUProcessor, ConllUProcessor) and Conllu (SinglePassConlluProcessor)
ConllUProcessor.processCoNLLUSentence() uses the generic 'process' verb; the method body calls indexer.addWord() for each token — it is indexing, not processing in any abstract sense

BlackLabQueryExecutor has six private methods all prefixed extractCollocateFrom*: extractCollocateFromXmlByPosition, extractCollocateFromMatchText, extractCollocateFromPattern, extractCollocateFromSnippet, extractCollocateFromMatch, extractCollocateFromPlainText — the repeated prefix obscures the significant difference in input format

StatisticsConverter and StatisticsIndexBuilder both deal with statistics persistence but the distinction between 'Converter' (TSV->binary) and 'IndexBuilder' (in-memory -> binary) is not immediately obvious from the names

strengths: The predicate hierarchy uses a rigidly consistent naming pattern (LemmaPredicate, TagPredicate, PosGroupPredicate, WordPredicate, AndPredicate, OrPredicate, NotPredicate) — a developer can predict any new predicate name immediately
guessXPOS and guessUPOS use the 'guess' prefix honestly, signalling that the conversion is heuristic and not authoritative
Binary I/O classes in indexer.hybrid follow a clear role-suffix convention: LemmaIdsCodec, LemmaLexiconReader, LemmaLexiconWriter, StatisticsReader — the role is always legible
SemanticFieldExplorer, CollocationsBuilder, LogDiceCalculator, CQLParser all use precise domain vocabulary with no ambiguity
encode/decode/decodeTo in LemmaIdsCodec are well-chosen: decodeTo communicates reuse-buffer semantics that decode does not
naming_quality.issue_character: Issues are concentrated in two areas: a vocabulary inconsistency around CoNLL-U processor class naming (inconsistent capitalization and mismatched conceptual axes for near-duplicate classes) and a proliferation of same-prefixed private helper names in BlackLabQueryExecutor; neither pattern is codebase-wide.
naming_quality.score_rationale: The codebase sits solidly above the 'significant drag' threshold (60) because the broad naming vocabulary is domain-appropriate and the predicate/codec subsystems are exemplary. What pulls the score down from 80+ is a clear naming dysfunction in the CoNLL-U processor cluster — two near-duplicate classes in the same package named along inconsistent axes with divergent capitalization of the same abbreviation — and a minor proliferation of ambiguous extractCollocateFrom* helpers in the query layer. These issues would slow a new developer navigating the indexer but would not derail comprehension of the overall system.

issues.summary: CoNLL-U is spelled two different ways across class names in the same package
evidence: HybridConllUProcessor (indexer.hybrid) uses 'ConllU'
SinglePassConlluProcessor (indexer.hybrid, same package) uses 'Conllu' — lowercase 'u'
ConllUProcessor (tagging) uses 'ConllU' matching the first convention
issues.suggestion: Standardise on 'ConlluProcessor' (all-lowercase after 'Conll') or 'ConllUProcessor' (capital U) codebase-wide. The Lucene/Java convention for acronym suffixes favours a single capital letter boundary, so 'ConllUProcessor' is the better choice; rename SinglePassConlluProcessor to SinglePassConllUProcessor.

issues.summary: HybridConllUProcessor and SinglePassConlluProcessor are named along different conceptual axes despite doing the same work in the same package
evidence: 'Hybrid' in HybridConllUProcessor refers to the output index type, not the processing strategy
'SinglePass' in SinglePassConlluProcessor refers to the algorithmic strategy, not the output type
Both classes have identical public APIs (processFile, processDirectory, setCommitInterval, close) and both delegate to HybridIndexer
The Javadoc for SinglePassConlluProcessor notes 'This class no longer produces collocations.bin' suggesting it supersedes HybridConllUProcessor, but a developer cannot infer this from the names
issues.suggestion: If one class supersedes the other, delete the obsolete one and give the survivor a name that expresses the output type consistently, e.g., HybridIndexConlluProcessor or simply HybridConllUIndexer. If both are intentionally kept for different call sites, add a deprecation annotation to the obsolete class and document in its Javadoc which should be used.

issues.summary: ConllUProcessor.processCoNLLUSentence() uses the generic 'process' verb for a method that exclusively indexes tokens into Lucene
evidence: processCoNLLUSentence (line 145) calls indexer.addWord() for every token in the sentence — it indexes, it does not 'process' in any general sense
The dimension rubric explicitly flags 'process' as a generic verb that reveals nothing
issues.suggestion: Rename to indexCoNLLUSentence to match the actual behaviour (indexing tokens into Lucene). This makes the read path obvious: processFile → indexCoNLLUSentence → indexer.addWord.

issues.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor has six private methods all named extractCollocateFrom*, leaving callers unable to distinguish their semantics from the name alone
evidence: extractCollocateFromXmlByPosition(xmlSnippet, position) — parses XML attribute at ordinal position
extractCollocateFromMatchText(matchText, position) — splits plain-text tokens by whitespace
extractCollocateFromPattern(bcqlPattern, hitIndex) — returns 'unknown'; placeholder
extractCollocateFromSnippet(snippet) — takes last lemma attribute from XML
extractCollocateFromMatch(matchOnly, labelPos) — (line 762) parses match string by label position
extractCollocateFromPlainText(text, position) — (line 787) splits plain text
Six methods share the same prefix yet differ in input format, return strategy, and completeness
issues.suggestion: Rename to encode the input contract in the suffix: extractLemmaAtPositionFromXml, extractLemmaAtPositionFromTokens, extractLastLemmaFromXmlSnippet, etc. This eliminates the need for callers to read the body to understand which overload applies.

root_causes: The CoNLL-U processor cluster was likely grown incrementally — each new class added a slightly different naming perspective (algorithm vs output type) without reconciling the existing class, leaving parallel classes with inconsistent names
The 'process' verb in ConllUProcessor may be a legacy of the class predating the indexer abstraction; the method body was updated to call addWord but the name was never revised to match
likely_symptoms: conllu-capitalisation-divergence is a symptom of the broader conllu_naming root cause rather than an independent problem
